channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Markets in Asia Plummet as Energy Fears Offset A.I. Optimism
item.description: Stocks in Asia sank on Wednesday, led by sharp declines in South Korea, as the war in Iran raised concerns about spiraling energy costs.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis and Meaghan Tobin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jung Yeon-Je/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Currency dealers monitor exchange rates as an electronic screen shows South Korea’s benchmark stock index (KOSPI) and the Korean Securities Dealers Automated Quotations (KOSDAQ) in a foreign exchange dealing room at the Hana Bank headquarters in Seoul on Wednesday.

item.title: Qué significa immutable
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 133 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Un oficio ancestral en peligro: la cerámica artesanal de Michoacán
item.description: La agricultura industrial y el crimen organizado presionan a una industria tradicional que durante generaciones sostuvo a los artesanos locales.
item.dc:creator: Michael Snyder and César Rodríguez

media:content.media:description: Cerámica vidriada del taller de Francisca Agustín Cristóbal

item.title: Word of the Day: immutable
item.description: This word has appeared in 133 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Tony Gonzales’s Scandal Forces Him Into Runoff Election in South Texas
item.description: Representative Gonzales did not get 50 percent or more from voters in the Republican primary race in Texas’ 23rd Congressional District, as required by law to win the nomination. He has battled allegations that he had an inappropriate relationship with a former staff member.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Tony Gonzales at a House Committee on Homeland Security hearing in Washington, D.C., last month.

item.title: Dan Crenshaw Loses to Steve Toth for Texas District 2
item.description: The victory by Steve Toth, a hard-line Texas state representative, underscored how even a conservative House member could lose Republican voters by breaking with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Dan Crenshaw, Republican of Texas, faced a primary challenge from Steve Toth, one of the most conservative members of the Texas House.

item.title: Iran Live Updates: Funeral Proceedings Begin for Slain Supreme Leader
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who led Iran for more than three decades, was killed in the U.S.-Israeli attacks. His replacement could be named as early as Wednesday as conflict grips the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: There Is Nothing Inevitable About a Russian Victory in Ukraine
item.description: If Russia has all the cards, why has it achieved so little?
item.dc:creator: Lawrence Freedman

media:content.media:credit: Oksana Parafeniuk for The New York Times

item.title: We Are Finally Free From Khamenei’s Suffocating Gaze
item.description: Now that Ayatollah Khamenei is dead, will another face replace his and carry on a version of the same story?
item.dc:creator: Azadeh Moaveni

media:content.media:credit: Abbas/Magnum Photos

item.title: Republican Primary for N.C. State Senate Seat Remains Too Close to Call
item.description: But that did not stop Sam Page, a small-town sheriff steeped in President Trump’s political movement, from declaring victory in the race.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina

item.title: North Carolina State Senate District 26 Primary Election Results 2026: Berger vs. Page
item.description: Get live results from the 2026 North Carolina primary election.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: What to Know About Nepal's Gen Z Election
item.description: Nepal is holding its first election since a youth-led uprising against corruption toppled the government last year. Here is what to know.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Binod Ghimire

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People attending a campaign rally in Kathmandu, Nepal, on Saturday.

item.title: Las redadas migratorias de Trump generan refugios clandestinos
item.description: En Springfield, Ohio, algunos estadounidenses han convertido sus sótanos y habitaciones libres en refugios para familias migrantes que podrían ser objeto de redadas.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan and Maddie McGarvey

media:content.media:description: Un dormitorio libre en una casa de Springfield, Ohio, preparada para personas haitianas, en caso de que los migrantes no se sintieran seguros en sus propios hogares.

item.title: Chimpanzees Are Really Into Crystals
item.description: In an attempt to understand our own fascination with the shiny minerals, researchers gave some to chimps.
item.dc:creator: Cara Giaimo

item.title: Corrections: March 4, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Wednesday, March 4, 2026.

item.title: Where are China’s A.I. Doomers?
item.description: Chinese policymakers and the public have expressed high levels of optimism about A.I., even as many in the West worry about the technology’s effects on employment or humanity in general.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Service robots on display at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai last year.

item.title: Quote of the Day: STRIKES KILLED IRAN SUCCESSORS EYED BY U.S.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, March 4, 2026.

item.title: Court Rulings Throw Dallas County Vote Tally Into Uncertainty
item.description: Hours after a district judge ordered the county to keep its polling places open, the Texas Supreme Court temporarily blocked the decision.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning, Nick Corasaniti and Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Emil T. Lippe for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A polling site at Samuell Grand Recreation Center in Dallas, on Tuesday.

item.title: Bobby Pulido Wins Democratic Primary in Texas’ 15th District
item.description: Mr. Pulido, a centrist and Latin Grammy Award-winning Tejano singer, defeated Ada Cuellar, an emergency room doctor who challenged his center-left views on abortion and connections to the Democratic establishment.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bobby Pulido will face Representative Monica de la Cruz for Texas’s 15th Congressional District, after winning Tuesday’s Democratic primary.

item.title: EE. UU. anuncia una operación militar en Ecuador contra el ‘narcoterrorismo’
item.description: El Comando Sur de EE. UU. dijo que la operación conjunta con Ecuador era un esfuerzo para “combatir la lacra del narcoterrorismo”, pero no aportó otros detalles sobre la operación.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Marcos Pin/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Integrantes de la Armada ecuatoriana montan guardia en las instalaciones del Comando de Guardacostas del Puerto de Manta, Ecuador, en 2025.

item.title: Cornyn and Paxton Head to Runoff in Bitter Texas Senate Primary
item.description: Neither Senator John Cornyn nor Attorney General Ken Paxton secured a majority on Tuesday, setting up a May runoff after a bitter and costly Republican primary.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Neither Senator John Cornyn, Republican of Texas, left, nor Ken Paxton, the Texas attorney general, secured a majority of votes on Tuesday in their contest for the Republican nomination for Mr. Cornyn’s Senate seat.

item.title: The Great Lie of War
item.description: In attacking Iran and Venezuela, Trump appears to believe he can decapitate regimes without events spinning out of control. What if he’s wrong? The contributing Opinion writer Ben Rhodes and the columnist Ezra Klein discuss what Trump underestimates about war on “The Ezra Klein Show.”
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 4, 2026
item.description: Wayne Bergman’s first solo puzzle for The New York Times leaves nothing to be desired.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Fadel Senna/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: It’s not a stretch to say that today’s crossword is brilliant.

item.title: Toronto Synagogue Is Damaged by Gunfire on Night of Jewish Holiday
item.description: The temple said 20 shots were fired at the building but no one was hurt.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

item.title: Democrats Question Credentials of Armed Squad Created by Trump Ally
item.description: Bruce Blakeman, the Republican candidate for governor of New York, formed a band of special deputies. A lawsuit calls the unit’s members unqualified and says that some appear to have arrest records.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan

media:content.media:credit: Graham Dickie/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bruce Blakeman, the Nassau County executive, is a close ally of President Trump.

item.title: U.S. Takes Military Action in Ecuador Against ‘Terrorist Organizations’
item.description: U.S. Special Forces soldiers are advising and supporting Ecuadorian commandos on raids across the country against suspected drug shipment facilities and other drug-related sites.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Luis Ferré-Sadurní
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Marcos Pin/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Members of the Ecuadorian Navy stand guard in the facilities of the Coast Guard Command at the Port of Manta, Ecuador, in 2025.

item.title: Congress Splits Over Iran War as Senate Faces a Vote
item.description: The administration’s shifting justifications for the military operation alarmed Democrats, who said no clear rationale had been given. Republicans struggled to echo the evolving explanations.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters on Tuesday about the conflict with Iran.

item.title: Roy Cooper and Michael Whatley Advance in North Carolina Senate Race
item.description: North Carolina has broken national Democrats’ hearts for nearly two decades, but this time they hope that Roy Cooper, the former governor, can once again find a path to victory.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times; Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Roy Cooper, left, the former Democratic governor of North Carolina, and Michael Whatley, the former chairman of the Republican National Committee.

item.title: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Communication Infrastructure in Mideast
item.description: Satellite images show damage near vital equipment on sites in at least five countries.
item.dc:creator: Devon Lum, Haley Willis and Riley Mellen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Carney Says That Canada’s Support for Airstrikes Came ‘With Regret’
item.description: The prime minister criticized Israel and the U.S. for not consulting other nations or the U.N. before their attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ian Austen/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, said Iran had persisted in its nuclear ambitions in defiance of years of diplomatic efforts, but he also criticized the United States and Israel.

item.title: Mapping Damage to Iran’s Ballistic Missile Sites With Satellite Images
item.description: A New York Times analysis finds evidence of strikes on Iran’s underground ballistic missile sites and facilities targeted last June.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados, Daniel Wood and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A building at a missile facility near Isfahan appears to be severely damaged.

item.title: Who Is Mojtaba Khamenei, the Son and Possible Successor of Iran’s Supreme Leader?
item.description: The selection of the son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on his compound on Saturday, could anger Iranians seeking change.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Office of The Iranian Supreme Leader/Wana News Agency, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A handout image from Iran state media of Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: These American Service Members Died in the Iran Conflict
item.description: They were from Nebraska, Florida, Iowa and Minnesota. One was wrapping up his final deployment and hoped to open a martial arts studio.
item.dc:creator: Corina Knoll, Christina Morales, Pooja Salhotra and Susan C. Beachy
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: United States Army Reserve

item.title: Senators Question Kristi Noem on ICE Immigration Tactics
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem repeatedly refused to apologize for suggesting that Alex Pretti and Renee Good, two U.S. citizens shot and killed by agents, were domestic terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kelso and Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Reuters

item.title: Is This the Year Texas Goes Blue?
item.description: The signs are familiar: an unpopular Republican president, well-funded Democrats and anecdotes of disaffected G.O.P. and independent voters.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein

item.title: Tracking Trump’s Endorsements in Tonight’s Primary Results: Texas, North Carolina and Arkansas
item.description: President Trump has endorsed hundreds of Republicans ahead of the midterm primary elections. See who is winning and losing.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: 9 Million Indians Live in the Persian Gulf. The Money They Send Home Is a Lifeline.
item.description: A huge number of Indians spend their lives hopping across the Arabian Sea — remitting billions of dollars a year, supporting their families and the Indian economy.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Mohammed Mahjoub/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Indian laborers who take up positions in construction, transportation and home care to send money back home.

item.title: Surge of First-Time Democratic Primary Voters Adds Uncertainty to Texas Races
item.description: A surge of voters who had previously participated only in general elections turned out in the Democratic primary, a shift that could make key Texas races harder to predict.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters waited in line to cast their ballots at E.M. Daggett Elementary School in Fort Worth on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Has Been Sued 198 Times for Withholding Funding. It Hasn’t Stopped Him.
item.description: Immigration demands for highway dollars, D.E.I. rules for homeless grants: how Trump has tried to wield spending to get his way.
item.dc:creator: Emily Badger and Alicia Parlapiano

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: The House Races to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections
item.description: Democrats are playing defense on a redrawn House map in Texas, while incumbents in both parties face fierce primary challenges in Texas and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Al Green, a Democrat in Houston, is in danger of losing his redrawn seat in the 18th Congressional District.

item.title: Judge Extends Voting in Dallas County After Complaints on Confusion
item.description: The judge extended voting until 9 p.m. Democrats had argued that a rule change spurred by Republicans confused voters who went to the wrong polling places.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:credit: Emil T. Lippe for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Jasmine Crockett speaking to reporters outside Oak Lawn Library in Dallas on Tuesday.

item.title: Police Arrest a Masked Man Outside Ken Paxton Watch Party
item.description: Officers found ammunition when searching his car after reports of a suspicious person. He was arrested on traffic charges, the Dallas police said.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman and Lauren McGaughy

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Police officers recovered magazines of ammunition from a car apparently driven by the man taken into custody.

item.title: What to Know About Jasmine Crockett, the Texas Democratic Senate Candidate
item.description: The congresswoman, who has established herself as a firebrand among fellow Democratic leaders, is looking to get on the ballot for Senate in November.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Jasmine Crockett of Dallas is running in the Democratic primary for Senate.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About James Talarico, Democratic Senate Hopeful in Texas
item.description: A Presbyterian seminarian, the Democrat has put his faith at the forefront of his political pitch.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Talarico sprinkles biblical references into his political arguments.

item.title: When to Expect Primary Election Results
item.description: Voters in Texas, North Carolina and Arkansas kicked off the midterm contests on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson

media:content.media:credit: Jonathan Drake/Reuters
media:content.media:description: An election worker preparing absentee ballots in Raleigh, N.C., in 2024.

item.title: Trump Tries to Quiet Claims Among Supporters That Israel Dragged Him Into War
item.description: Many of President Trump’s allies have urged him and his Make America Great Again movement to shift away from their close ties to Israel and military entanglements in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump attempted to extinguish the idea that Israel forced the United States to enter a war with Iran.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Senate Candidate Ken Paxton of Texas
item.description: The Texas attorney general rode into office on a Tea Party wave and has faced several high-profile scandals during his tenure.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton has aligned himself with President Trump.

item.title: Election Live Updates: Democratic Senate Primary Still Undecided in Texas
item.description: The Democratic primary between Representative Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico was thrown into confusion amid legal challenges. On the Republican side, Senator John Cornyn, a four-term incumbent, was forced into a runoff with Ken Paxton.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
